title: Ensure clusters are created with Private Nodes

input_row.title: Ensure clusters are created with Private Nodes
input_row.description: Private Nodes are nodes with no public IP addresses. Disable public IP addresses for cluster nodes, so that they only have private IP addresses.
input_row.rationale: Disabling public IP addresses on cluster nodes restricts access to only internal networks, forcing attackers to obtain local network access before attempting to compromise the underlying Kubernetes hosts.
input_row.audit: Using Google Cloud Console: 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list. 2. Select the desired cluster, and within the Details pane, make sure Private Cluster is set to Enabled. Using Command Line: Run this command: gcloud container clusters describe $CLUSTER_NAME --zone $COMPUTE_ZONE -- format json | jq '.privateClusterConfig.enablePrivateNodes' The output of the above command returns true if Private Nodes is enabled.
input_row.remediation: Once a cluster is created without enabling Private Nodes, it cannot be remediated. Rather the cluster must be recreated. Using Google Cloud Console: Internal Only - General 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list. 2. Click CREATE CLUSTER. 3. Configure the cluster as required then click Networking under CLUSTER in the navigation pane. 4. Under IPv4 network access, click the Private cluster radio button. 5. Configure the other settings as required, and click CREATE. Using Command Line: To create a cluster with Private Nodes enabled, include the --enable-private-nodes flag within the cluster create command: gcloud container clusters create --location $LOCATION -- enable-private-nodes
input_row.impact: To enable Private Nodes, the cluster has to also be configured with a private master IP range and IP Aliasing enabled. Private Nodes do not have outbound access to the public internet. If you want to provide outbound Internet access for your private nodes, you can use Cloud NAT or you can manage your own NAT gateway. To access Google Cloud APIs and services from private nodes, Private Google Access needs to be set on Kubernetes Engine Cluster Subnets.
input_row.default_value: By default, Private Nodes are disabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit field provides a complete command using the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) to check whether Private Nodes are enabled (`gcloud container clusters describe $CLUSTER_NAME --zone $COMPUTE_ZONE --format json | jq '.privateClusterConfig.enablePrivateNodes'`).
- There are no indications that SSH access or manual verification is required since this can be fully automated through available APIs.
- Given the nature of checking cluster configurations, it aligns with standard GCP service properties.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_gke_cluster_private_nodes_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) command: `gcloud container clusters describe $CLUSTER_NAME --zone $COMPUTE_ZONE --format json` to retrieve the cluster configuration in JSON format.
- Parse the output to check the value of `.privateClusterConfig.enablePrivateNodes`.
- **Resources to enumerate:** GKE clusters across all relevant locations.
- **Configuration properties/settings to validate:** Ensure that the `.privateClusterConfig.enablePrivateNodes` property is set to `true`.
- **Pass/Fail criteria:** "Pass" if the value is `true`, indicating Private Nodes are enabled; "Fail" if the value is `false` or if the property does not exist.
